name: The Recursive I Ching
license: Mixed — see each grammar's own `license` field (public-domain classical text; original synthesis/interpretation CC-BY-SA-4.0)

branches.name: Primary Sources — the classical text itself, in translation
Synthesis — cross-lens readings of the 64 hexagrams
Readings — thematic contemplative practices, not interpretation sets

grammars.name: I Ching — Chinese Original (Project Gutenberg)
grammars.blurb: The classical Chinese source text -- Judgment, Image, and all six Line statements -- for each of the 64 hexagrams, character for character as digitized by Project Gutenberg (ebook #25501, 易經, catalogu…
grammars.year_label: Zhou I Ching, compiled c. 1046–256 BCE · classical Chinese, Project Gutenberg ebook #25501, no translation layer

grammars.name: The 64 Hexagrams
grammars.blurb: The Judgment, Image, and all six line texts for each of the 64 hexagrams, in condensed English, with a short interpretation gloss per hexagram. This is the flagship reading text of The Recursive I Chi…
grammars.year_label: Zhou I Ching, compiled c. 1046–256 BCE · this English condensation's own translation lineage is undocumented

grammars.name: The Repair I Ching: A Contemplative Practice
grammars.blurb: A meta-grammar that weaves the I Ching into the Repair framework. Twelve hexagrams — one for each chapter of 'Repair: An Open Source Book' — chosen not to predict but to reperceive. Each hexagram illu…

grammars.name: The Ten Wings — the Confucian Layer
grammars.blurb: The commentarial stratum of the I Ching, held apart as its own grammar so you can SEE the moralizing layer as an addition, not the origin. The Ten Wings (Shiyi) were composed by multiple hands in the …
grammars.year_label: Ten Wings, Confucian school, late Warring States–early Han, c. 350–100 BCE · English: James Legge 1882, public domain

grammars.name: Three Lenses for Viewing the 64 Hexagrams
grammars.blurb: This interpretation maps the 64 I Ching hexagrams to Human Design 'gates'—a synthesis channeled by Ra Uru Hu in 1987 after an eight-day encounter with 'the Voice' on Ibiza. The system is only about 30…

grammars.name: Zhouyi Core — the Oldest Layer
grammars.blurb: The bottom stratum of the I Ching: for each of the 64 hexagrams, the Judgment (guaci) and the six Line statements (yaoci) — and nothing else. No Image, no 'superior man', no moral philosophy: those be…
grammars.year_label: Zhouyi core (Judgment + Line statements), late Western Zhou, c. 9th century BCE (Shaughnessy) · English: James Legge 1882, public domain
